title: Semi-dry western blot and chemiluminescent detection
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"7cl2c","text":"Methanol: Repeated or prolonged contact with skin may cause dermatitis. The substance may have effects on the central nervous system , resulting in persistent or recurring headaches and impaired vision. Wear safety goggles.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare your samples and run SDS-PAGE as usual.
Dilute Timmons-Dunbar Buffer 1X using ultrapure water. Usually, 50 mL for each mini gel (8.4cm x 7cm) to be transfered are enough. Tip: Perform all steps from this section during SDS-PAGE run
Immediately transfer the membrane to 3% non-fat dry milk diluted in TBS-T (Tris Buffered Saline with 0.05% Tween 20) and incubate with gently rocking for 1 hour. 01:00:00, Membrane blocking

Mix 1 mL of 'Stable Peroxide Solution' with 1 mL of 'Luminol Enhancer Solution' in a clean container. SuperSignal™ West Pico PLUS Chemiluminescent Substrate Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #34577 Tip: Any chemiluminescent reagent can be used

Carefully and removing bubbles with an roller, assembly the following: Bottom electrode 6 x WypAll X60 buffer-soaked sheets Pre-wet PVDF membrane 6 x WypAll X60 buffer-soaked sheets Top electrode Follow the picture as an guideline: a
Soak 12 WypAll X60 sheets (cutted in gel size) in Timmons-Dunbar Buffer 1X. Wiper Wypall X60 Quartfold Kimberly-Clark Catalog #34865
Pre-wet PVDF membrane (cutted in gel size) for 30 seconds in 100% methanol Immun-Blot® PVDF Membrane 0,2µm Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #1620177 00:00:30, Membrane activation
Discard the methanol and overlay membrane with Timmons-Dunbar Buffer 1X Membrane will usually float
Remove gel from electrophoresis chamber and disassembly the sandwich Do not wash the gel! Remaining SDS is important for protein transfer.

Perform the transfer at 0.325 mA/mm 2 for 7 minutes when using 0.75mm mini gels. The following table (From Trans-Blot Turbo Blotting System (Cat #170-4155) manual) is quite usefull: %s %s Protocol Name MW (kDa) Time (min) 2 Mini Gels or 1 Midi Gel 1 Mini Format Gel 0.75 mm thick gels > 150 10 2.5 A; up to 25 V 1.3 A; up to 25 V Mixed 7 < 30 5 1.5 mm thick gels Any 10

Disassembly the sandwich and proceed with blocking and antibody incubation. Tip: Membranes can be stained with Ponceau S to check protein transfer and binding. Gels can be checked for protein leftover by Coomassie Blue staining.
Pour off washing solution and overlay membrane with TBS-T, let it gently rocking for 5 minutes (wash 2/3) 00:05:00, Wash (1/3)

Pour off washing solution and overlay with desired antibody. Incubate from 1 hour (room temperature) up to 16 hours (4°C) with gently rocking. Undetermined, for overnight (16h) incubations Tip: Follow manufacturer's instructions for primary antibody dilution. As a starting point, 1:1,000 dilution in TBS-T with 1% BSA (Bovine Serum Albumin) and 0.02% sodium azide (in order to prevent microorganism growth and antibody reuse).
Pour off blocking solution and overlay membrane with TBS-T, let it gently rocking for 5 minutes (wash 1/3) 00:05:00, Wash (1/3)
Pour off washing solution and overlay membrane with TBS-T, let it gently rocking for 5 minutes (wash 3/3) 00:05:00, Wash (1/3)
Collect antibody and store at 4°C for further uses. Overlay membrane with TBS-T, let it gently rocking for 5 minutes (wash 1/3). 00:05:00, Wash (2/3)
Pour off washing solution and overlay membrane with TBS-T, let it gently rocking for 5 minutes (wash 3/3). 00:05:00, Wash (2/3)
Pour off washing solution and overlay membrane with TBS-T, let it gently rocking for 5 minutes (wash 2/3). 00:05:00, Wash (2/3)
Gently transfer the membrane to this container using tweezers and rock for a few seconds. Tip: Grab the membrane by the border in order to avoid tweezer marks over the bands

Transfer the wet membrane to the X-ray cassete. The best approach is 'sandwich' it with two transparent hard plastic foils (as picture bellow). a Tip: X-ray cassetes with white enchancing screens give better results

Inside the dark room (remember to left your bright-screen cellphone outside), add one X-ray film over the assembled sandwich and expose for 1 minute. Tip 1: Increase or reduce exposure time as needed. Tip 2: Cheaper "hospital grade" X-ray films can be used in place of expensive "research grade" films.
Remove the film from cassete and put in a container with diluted autoradiography developer solution and incubate for 30 seconds. 00:00:30, Developer Tip: Replace solution as it becomes dark
Rinse with tap water
Put in a container with diluted autoradiography fixer solution and incubate for 1 minute 00:01:00, Fixer Tip: Replace solution as it becomes yellowish
Rinse with tap water and let it dry go to step #28
Overlay the dried film over the original cassete sandwich to tick the prestained marker, stick to blank sheet of paper and scan it.

Transfer buffer: Alcohol-free Timmons-Dunbar Weight reagents as indicated: 30.285 g of Tris-Base 14.41 g of Glycine Transfer the reagents to a beaker, add 150 mL of ultrapure water, and stir using amagnetic bar until complete dissolution. Using a volumetric flask, complete the volume to 250 mL with ultrapure water. Store at room temperature. Please cite Timmons TM, Dunbar BS. Protein blotting and immunodetection. Methods Enzymol (1990), 182:679-688. DOI: 10.1016/0076-6879(90)82053-5 Please cite: Timmons TM, Dunbar BS. Protein blotting and immunodetection. Methods Enzymol (1990), 182:679-688. DOI: 10.1016/0076-6879(90)82053-5 %s %s Timmons-Dunbar 4X Transfer Buffer Reagent Concentration Amount Tris-Base 1000 mM 30.285 g Glycine 768 mM 14.41 g Ultrapure water q.s. 250 mL Glycine Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #50046 Trizma® base Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #T1503-1KG

Pour off washing soution and overlay with horseradish peroxidase conjugated antibody. Incubate from 1-3 hours at room temperature with gently rocking. Tip: Follow manufacturer's instructions for primary antibody dilution. As a starting point, 1:10,000 dilution in TBS-T.
Pour off the secondary antibody. Overlay membrane with TBS-T, let it gently rocking for 5 minutes (wash 1/3). 00:05:00, Wash (2/3)
Pour off washing solution and overlay membrane with TBS-T, let it gently rocking for 5 minutes (wash 3/3). 00:05:00, Wash (2/3)
Pour off washing solution and overlay membrane with TBS-T, let it gently rocking for 5 minutes (wash 3/3). 00:05:00, Wash (2/3)

General Washing Buffer: Tris-buffered saline (TBS) 10X Weight reagents as indicated: 24,2 g of Tris-Base 87,6 g of Sodium chloride (NaCl) Transfer the reagents to a beaker, add 900 mL of ultrapure water, and stir using an magnetic bar until complete dissolution. Using a volumetric flask, complete the volume to 250 mL with ultrapure water. Store at room temperature.
creator: Douglas Adamoski
